#XQuery

XQuery is a full programming language for querying and constructing XML. Where XPath selects nodes from a document, XQuery can build entirely new documents from multiple sources — think of it as SQL for hierarchical data.

If XPath is SELECT column FROM table WHERE condition, XQuery is the full SQL with JOIN, subqueries, and INSERT INTO.

#What's Here

  • FLWOR Expressions — The core of XQuery: For, Let, Where, Order by, Return. Like LINQ query syntax but for XML.

  • Advanced FLWOR — Window clauses, count, group by, while, otherwise — advanced FLWOR patterns.

  • Constructors — Building new XML elements, attributes, and documents from query results.

  • Conditionals and Error Handling — if/then/else, switch, typeswitch, try/catch, and the otherwise operator.

  • Operators — Comparison, arithmetic, sequence, and navigation operators.

  • Type System — XQuery types, sequence types, records, enums, union types, and type testing.

  • Update Facility — XQuery Update — insert, delete, replace, rename, and transform expressions.

  • Functions and Modules — Writing reusable XQuery functions and organizing code into modules.

  • XQuery vs XSLT — When to use which. They overlap significantly — here's how to choose.

  • Full-Text Search — Linguistic search with stemming, wildcards, proximity, and scoring.

  • Prolog and Declarations — Module declarations, namespace imports, options, and configuration.

  • Extensibility — Custom functions, modules, external functions, and .NET integration.

#The LINQ Parallel

XQuery's FLWOR expression maps almost directly to LINQ query syntax:

XQuery: 

for $book in /catalog/book
where $book/price > 30
order by $book/title
return <result>{ $book/title, $book/price }</result>

LINQ: 

from book in catalog.Elements("book")
where (decimal)book.Element("price") > 30
orderby (string)book.Element("title")
select new XElement("result", book.Element("title"), book.Element("price"))

The XQuery version is more concise, handles namespaces natively, and runs on any XQuery processor — not just .NET.

Built with Crucible
